title: TÍTULO DE LA ENTRADA
text:
Nota metodológica
DESCRIPCIÓN de las variables, unidades, y criterios de filtrado aplicados.

title: Flujos globales de exportación de armas
text:
Ver código
library(circlize)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

flows <- read_csv("data/flows_import-export-top_2014-2025.csv")
flows <- flows %>% filter(pct >= 20)

col_exporters <- c(
  "United States"  = "#2B7BAD",
  "Russia"         = "#AD3B3B",
  "France"         = "#3B6BAD",
  "Germany"        = "#4A8A4A",
  "China"          = "#B5621A",
  "United Kingdom" = "#6B4AAD"
)

all_nodes <- unique(c(flows$from, flows$to))
grid_col <- setNames(rep("#A8A8A8", length(all_nodes)), all_nodes)
grid_col[names(col_exporters)] <- col_exporters

importers_only <- setdiff(all_nodes, names(col_exporters))
import_cols <- colorRampPalette(c("#7AAFC4", "#6A9DB8", "#5A8BAA"))(length(importers_only))
grid_col[importers_only] <- import_cols

par(mar = c(0, 0, 0, 0))
circos.clear()
circos.par(
  gap.after = c(rep(2, length(all_nodes) - 1), 8),
  canvas.xlim = c(-1.1, 1.1),
  canvas.ylim = c(-1.1, 1.1)
)

chordDiagram(
  flows,
  grid.col = grid_col,
  transparency = 0.15,
  directional = 1,
  direction.type = "arrows",
  link.arr.type = "big.arrow",
  annotationTrack = "grid",
  preAllocateTracks = list(track.height = 0.05)
)

circos.trackPlotRegion(track.index = 1, panel.fun = function(x, y) {
  xlim <- get.cell.meta.data("xlim")
  ylim <- get.cell.meta.data("ylim")
  sector.name <- get.cell.meta.data("sector.index")
  circos.text(mean(xlim), ylim[1] + 0.5, sector.name,
              facing = "clockwise", niceFacing = TRUE,
              adj = c(0, 0.5), cex = 0.65, col = "grey20")
}, bg.border = NA)

title: Flujos globales de exportación de armas
text:
Ver código
library(circlize)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

flows <- read_csv("data/flows_import-export-top_2014-2025.csv")
flows <- flows %>% filter(pct >= 20)

col_exporters <- c(
  "United States"  = "#2B7BAD",
  "Russia"         = "#AD3B3B",
  "France"         = "#3B6BAD",
  "Germany"        = "#4A8A4A",
  "China"          = "#B5621A",
  "United Kingdom" = "#6B4AAD"
)

all_nodes <- unique(c(flows$from, flows$to))
grid_col <- setNames(rep("#A8A8A8", length(all_nodes)), all_nodes)
grid_col[names(col_exporters)] <- col_exporters

importers_only <- setdiff(all_nodes, names(col_exporters))
import_cols <- colorRampPalette(c("#7AAFC4", "#6A9DB8", "#5A8BAA"))(length(importers_only))
grid_col[importers_only] <- import_cols

par(mar = c(0, 0, 0, 0))
circos.clear()
circos.par(
  gap.after = c(rep(2, length(all_nodes) - 1), 8),
  canvas.xlim = c(-1.1, 1.1),
  canvas.ylim = c(-1.1, 1.1)
)

chordDiagram(
  flows,
  grid.col = grid_col,
  transparency = 0.15,
  directional = 1,
  direction.type = "arrows",
  link.arr.type = "big.arrow",
  annotationTrack = "grid",
  preAllocateTracks = list(track.height = 0.05)
)

circos.trackPlotRegion(track.index = 1, panel.fun = function(x, y) {
  xlim <- get.cell.meta.data("xlim")
  ylim <- get.cell.meta.data("ylim")
  sector.name <- get.cell.meta.data("sector.index")
  circos.text(mean(xlim), ylim[1] + 0.5, sector.name,
              facing = "clockwise", niceFacing = TRUE,
              adj = c(0, 0.5), cex = 0.65, col = "grey20")
}, bg.border = NA)

title: Flujos globales de exportación de armas
text:
Fuente
SIPRI Arms Transfers Database. Disponible en: sipri.org/databases/armstransfers

title: Flujos globales de exportación de armas
text:
Nota metodológica
Los flujos representan transferencias de armas mayores expresadas en millones de TIV (Trend Indicator Values), unidad propia de SIPRI que mide el valor de transferencia según capacidad militar del sistema, no precio de mercado. Se incluyen únicamente los flujos que superan el 20% de las importaciones totales de cada receptor en el periodo 2014-2025.

title: Gasto en defensa de los países de la OTAN
text:
Ver código
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

raw <- read_excel("data/SIPRI-Milex-data-1949-2025_v1.2.xlsx", 
                  sheet = "Share of GDP",
                  skip = 5)

otan <- c("United States of America", "United Kingdom", "France", "Germany", "Italy",
          "Spain", "Poland", "Netherlands", "Belgium", "Portugal", "Norway",
          "Denmark", "Greece", "Türkiye", "Romania", "Hungary", "Czechia",
          "Slovakia", "Bulgaria", "Croatia", "Albania", "Montenegro",
          "North Macedonia", "Latvia", "Lithuania", "Estonia", "Luxembourg",
          "Slovenia", "Canada")

nato_long <- raw %>%
  filter(Country %in% otan) %>%
  select(Country, `2014`:`2024`) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = `2014`:`2024`, names_to = "year", values_to = "gdp_share") %>%
  mutate(gdp_share = as.numeric(gdp_share) * 100, year = as.integer(year))

ggplot(nato_long, aes(x = year, y = reorder(Country, gdp_share), fill = gdp_share)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#f7fcf5", high = "#005a32", na.value = "grey90", name = "% PIB") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2014:2024, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.4) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
        legend.position = "none")

title: Gasto en defensa de los países de la OTAN
text:
Ver código
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

raw <- read_excel("data/SIPRI-Milex-data-1949-2025_v1.2.xlsx", 
                  sheet = "Share of GDP",
                  skip = 5)

otan <- c("United States of America", "United Kingdom", "France", "Germany", "Italy",
          "Spain", "Poland", "Netherlands", "Belgium", "Portugal", "Norway",
          "Denmark", "Greece", "Türkiye", "Romania", "Hungary", "Czechia",
          "Slovakia", "Bulgaria", "Croatia", "Albania", "Montenegro",
          "North Macedonia", "Latvia", "Lithuania", "Estonia", "Luxembourg",
          "Slovenia", "Canada")

nato_long <- raw %>%
  filter(Country %in% otan) %>%
  select(Country, `2014`:`2024`) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = `2014`:`2024`, names_to = "year", values_to = "gdp_share") %>%
  mutate(gdp_share = as.numeric(gdp_share) * 100, year = as.integer(year))

ggplot(nato_long, aes(x = year, y = reorder(Country, gdp_share), fill = gdp_share)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#f7fcf5", high = "#005a32", na.value = "grey90", name = "% PIB") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2014:2024, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.4) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
        legend.position = "none")

title: Gasto en defensa de los países de la OTAN
text:
Fuente
SIPRI Military Expenditure Database. Disponible en: sipri.org/databases/milex

title: Gasto en defensa de los países de la OTAN
text:
Nota metodológica
El indicador representa el gasto en defensa de cada país como porcentaje de su PIB. Un valor alto no implica necesariamente mayor capacidad militar absoluta, sino mayor esfuerzo relativo respecto al tamaño de la economía.

title: Fuentes de datos
text:

Cobertura: 1949–2025, todos los países
Descargado: Junio 2026
Usado en: 01-nato-defense-spending
URL: sipri.org/databases/milex





Variable: Transferencias de armas mayores en millones de TIV
Cobertura: 2014–2025, top 20 importadores
Descargado: Junio 2026
Usado en: 02-arms-transfers
URL: sipri.org/databases/armstransfers
